user_message: This just came in from Laura Nguyen at Adatum Corporation. Can you identify the blockers?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 10:16 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with consumption trend. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 10:31 EST
Subject: consumption trend - Action Required

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 10:11 EST
Subject: Re: consumption trend Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-C trap (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-C failure mode (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-C -- Skips the 'waiting on whom' question
